raw:
```json
{
  "criterion": "api_design",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "OpenAPI validates cleanly (`is valid`) and all listed endpoints exist in the implementation. URL shapes follow REST conventions: nouns, plural, hierarchical (`/api/loans`, `/api/loans/:id/return`, `/api/reservations/:id/cancel`, `/api/fines/:id/pay`) — no verb-style URLs like `/api/borrowBook`. Status codes match REQUIREMENTS expectations: 402 for outstanding fines, 403 for suspended members, 409 for loan limit / no copies / duplicates, 201 for creates, 200 for mutations of existing resources (probe confirms `POST /api/loans`→201, `404` on unknown member/book GETs, `400` on bad bodies). Bodies are validated with zod and return 400 with field-level issue arrays (probe: `POST /api/books` empty body → 400 with per-field zod issues). Error shape is consistently `{\"error\": ...}` across handlers and is documented in `components.schemas.Error`. This puts it solidly at level 3.\n\nIt does not reach 4 because: (a) error responses carry only a human message — no stable `code` field like `LOAN_LIMIT_REACHED` for programmatic handling; (b) OpenAPI carries no examples for the negative ACs; (c) the documented `Error.error` is `string`, but validation errors actually return an array of zod issues, so the documented schema does not faithfully describe the wire format; (d) `POST /api/books` is documented as returning `Book`, but the probe shows it actually returns `BookDetail` (`reservation_queue_depth: 0` is present); (e) no 400 response is documented on any body-accepting endpoint. It also does not reach 5 because error-to-HTTP mapping is not centralized — each handler repeats its own `instanceof ConflictError/ForbiddenError/PaymentRequiredError` ladder (loan-handler.ts:29-41, reservation-handler.ts:29-33), and the only middleware-level handler in routes.ts:40-43 just catches anything else and returns generic 500. Schemas are also loose (no explicit `additionalProperties: false`, several `nullable: true` fields). The `expireStaleReservations` op is at least exposed as a clean POST `/api/reservations/expire` returning `{expired: n}`, which is a positive."
}
```

parsed.rationale:
OpenAPI validates cleanly (`is valid`) and all listed endpoints exist in the implementation. URL shapes follow REST conventions: nouns, plural, hierarchical (`/api/loans`, `/api/loans/:id/return`, `/api/reservations/:id/cancel`, `/api/fines/:id/pay`) — no verb-style URLs like `/api/borrowBook`. Status codes match REQUIREMENTS expectations: 402 for outstanding fines, 403 for suspended members, 409 for loan limit / no copies / duplicates, 201 for creates, 200 for mutations of existing resources (probe confirms `POST /api/loans`→201, `404` on unknown member/book GETs, `400` on bad bodies). Bodies are validated with zod and return 400 with field-level issue arrays (probe: `POST /api/books` empty body → 400 with per-field zod issues). Error shape is consistently `{"error": ...}` across handlers and is documented in `components.schemas.Error`. This puts it solidly at level 3.

It does not reach 4 because: (a) error responses carry only a human message — no stable `code` field like `LOAN_LIMIT_REACHED` for programmatic handling; (b) OpenAPI carries no examples for the negative ACs; (c) the documented `Error.error` is `string`, but validation errors actually return an array of zod issues, so the documented schema does not faithfully describe the wire format; (d) `POST /api/books` is documented as returning `Book`, but the probe shows it actually returns `BookDetail` (`reservation_queue_depth: 0` is present); (e) no 400 response is documented on any body-accepting endpoint. It also does not reach 5 because error-to-HTTP mapping is not centralized — each handler repeats its own `instanceof ConflictError/ForbiddenError/PaymentRequiredError` ladder (loan-handler.ts:29-41, reservation-handler.ts:29-33), and the only middleware-level handler in routes.ts:40-43 just catches anything else and returns generic 500. Schemas are also loose (no explicit `additionalProperties: false`, several `nullable: true` fields). The `expireStaleReservations` op is at least exposed as a clean POST `/api/reservations/expire` returning `{expired: n}`, which is a positive.
